core_mechanism: history-dependent adaptive-bias flattening of a probability/energy landscape along a few coarse coordinates, with a self-attenuating (tempered) deposition rate that makes the accumulated bias converge to a controlled, scaled reconstruction of the underlying landscape
brief_reason: The abstract builds the landscape along selected collective variables using an adaptive bias whose tuning interpolates between full flattening and unbiased sampling (the two limiting cases) while giving rigorously controlled convergence, which is exactly a tempered, history-dependent adaptive-biasing scheme that reconstructs the free-energy surface.
